name: pytest-lsp

metadata.name: pytest-lsp
metadata.summary: A pytest plugin for end-to-end testing of language servers

documentation:
# pytest-lsp: End-to-end testing of language servers with pytest

`pytest-lsp` is a pytest plugin for writing end-to-end tests for language servers.

It works by running the language server in a subprocess and communicating with it over stdio, just like a real language client.
This also means `pytest-lsp` can be used to test language servers written in any language - not just Python.

`pytest-lsp` relies on the [`pygls`](https://github.com/openlawlibrary/pygls) library for its language server protocol implementation.

See the [documentation](https://lsp-devtools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) for details on getting started.

```python
import sys

import pytest_lsp
from lsprotocol.types import (
    CompletionParams,
    InitializeParams,
    Position,
    TextDocumentIdentifier,
)
from pytest_lsp import (
    ClientServerConfig,
    LanguageClient,
    client_capabilities,
)


@pytest_lsp.fixture(
    config=ClientServerConfig(
        server_command=[sys.executable, "-m", "esbonio"],
    ),
)
async def client(lsp_client: LanguageClient):
    # Setup
    response = await lsp_client.initialize_session(
        InitializeParams(
            capabilities=client_capabilities("visual-studio-code"),
            root_uri="file:///path/to/test/project/root/",
        )
    )

    yield

    # Teardown
    await lsp_client.shutdown_session()


async def test_completion(client: LanguageClient):
    result = await client.text_document_completion_async(
        params=CompletionParams(
            position=Position(line=5, character=23),
            text_document=TextDocumentIdentifier(
                uri="file:///path/to/test/project/root/test_file.rst"
            ),
        )
    )

    assert len(result.items) > 0
```

modules.name: pytest_lsp.checks
documentation:
Checks to ensure that the server is compliant with the spec.

These can be used in your unit tests to ensure that objects (such as `CompletionItem`
etc) take into account the client's capabilities when constructed.

However, they are also called automatically during end-to-end tests that make use of the
standard {class}`~pytest_lsp.LanguageClient`. See {ref}`pytest-lsp-spec-checks` for more
details.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.check_result_for
documentation: Define a result check.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.check_params_of
documentation: Define a params check.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.check_result_against_client_capabilities
documentation:
Check that the given result respects the client's declared capabilities.

This will emit an `LspSpecificationWarning` if any issues are detected.

# Parameters

capabilities

: The client's capabilities

method

: The method name to validate the result of

result

: The result to validate

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.check_params_against_client_capabilities
documentation:
Check that the given params respect the client's declared capabilities.

This will emit an `LspSpecificationWarning` if any issues are detected.

# Parameters

capabilities

: The client's capabilities

method

: The method name to validate the result of

params

: The params to validate

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.check_completion_item
documentation: Ensure that the given `CompletionItem` complies with the given capabilities.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.completion_items
documentation:
Ensure that the completion items returned from the server are compliant with the
spec and the client's declared capabilities.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.completion_item_resolve
documentation:
Ensure that the completion item returned from the server is compliant with the
spec and the client's declared capbabilities.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.document_links
documentation:
Ensure that the document links returned from the server are compliant with the
Spec and the client's declared capabilities.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.work_done_progress_create
documentation:
Assert that the client has support for `window/workDoneProgress/create`
requests.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.checks.workspace_configuration
documentation: Ensure that the client has support for `workspace/configuration` requests.

classes.name: pytest_lsp.checks.LspSpecificationWarning
documentation: Warning raised when encountering results that fall outside the spec.

classes.name: pytest_lsp.protocol.LanguageClientProtocol

methods.name: pytest_lsp.protocol.LanguageClientProtocol._handle_request
documentation:
Wrap pygls' handle_request implementation. This will

- Check if the request from the server is compatible with the client's stated
  capabilities.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.protocol.LanguageClientProtocol._handle_notification
documentation:
Wrap pygls' handle_notification implementation. This will

- Notify a future waiting on the notification, if applicable.

- Check the params to see if they are compatible with the client's stated
  capabilities.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.protocol.LanguageClientProtocol.send_request_async
documentation:
Wrap pygls' `send_request_async` implementation. This will

- Check the params to see if they're compatible with the client's stated
  capabilities
- Check the result to see if it's compatible with the client's stated
  capabilities

# Parameters

method

: The method name of the request to send

params

: The associated parameters to go with the request

# Returns

Any

: The result

methods.name: pytest_lsp.protocol.LanguageClientProtocol.wait_for_notification
documentation:
Wait for a notification message with the given `method`.

# Parameters

method

: The method name to wait for

callback

: If given, `callback` will be called with the notification message's
  `params` when recevied

# Returns

Future

: > A future that will resolve when the requested notification message is

  recevied.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.protocol.LanguageClientProtocol.wait_for_notification_async
documentation:
Wait for a notification message with the given `method`.

# Parameters

method

: The method name to wait for

# Returns

Any

: The notification message's `params`

documentation: type: ignore[assignment]
An extended protocol class adding functionality useful for testing.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.pytest_runtest_makereport
documentation: Add any captured log messages to the report.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.get_fixture_arguments
documentation:
Return the arguments to pass to the user's fixture function.

Parameters
----------
fn
   The user's fixture function

client
   The test client instance to inject

request
   pytest's request fixture

Returns
-------
dict
   The set of arguments to pass to the user's fixture function

functions.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.fixture
documentation:
Define a fixture that returns a client connected to a server running in a
background sub-process

Parameters
----------
config
   Configuration for the client and server.

documentation: Get the lsp-devtools command required to connect to the given ``server``

methods.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.ClientServerConfig.get_server_command
documentation: Get the command to start the server with.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.ClientServerConfig.start
documentation:
Return the client instance to use for the test.

# Parameters

devtools

: If set, enable `lsp-devtools` integration, should be of the form
  `<port>` or `<host>:<port>`. Where `<host>` and `<port>`
  describe how to connect to an `lsp-devtools` server program.

# Returns

JsonRPCClient

: The client instance to use in the test.

class_variables.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.ClientServerConfig.server_command
documentation: The command to use to start the language server.

class_variables.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.ClientServerConfig.client_factory
documentation: Factory function to use when constructing the test client instance.

class_variables.name: pytest_lsp.plugin.ClientServerConfig.server_env
documentation: Environment variables to set when starting the server.
Configuration for a Client-Server connection.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.client.cancel_all_tasks
documentation: Called to cancel all awaited tasks.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.client.make_test_lsp_client
documentation:
Construct a new test client instance with the handlers needed to capture
additional responses from the server.

functions.name: pytest_lsp.client.client_capabilities
documentation:
Find the capabilities that correspond to the given client spec.

This function supports the following syntax

`client-name` or `client-name@latest`

: Return the capabilities of the latest version of `client-name`

`client-name@v2`

: Return the latest release of the `v2` of `client-name`

`client-name@v2.3.1`

: Return exactly `v2.3.1` of `client-name`

# Parameters

client_spec

: The string describing the client to load the corresponding
  capabilities for.

# Raises

ValueError

: If the requested client's capabilities could not be found

# Returns

ClientCapabilities

: The requested client capabilities

classes.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient

methods.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient.server_exit
documentation: Called when the server process exits.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient.report_server_error
documentation:
Called when the server does something unexpected, e.g. sending malformed
JSON.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient.get_configuration
documentation:
Get a configuration value.

# Parameters

section

: The optional section name to retrieve.
  If `None` the top level configuration object for the requested scope will
  be returned

scope_uri

: The scope at which to set the configuration.
  If `None`, this will default to the global scope.

# Returns

Optional\[Any\]

: The requested configuration value or `None` if not found.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient.set_configuration
documentation:
Set a configuration value.

# Parameters

item

: The value to set

section

: The optional section name to set.
  If `None` the top level configuration object will be overriden with
  `item`.

scope_uri

: The scope at which to set the configuration.
  If `None`, this will default to the global scope.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient.initialize_session
documentation:
Make an `initialize` request to a lanaguage server.

It will also automatically send an `initialized` notification once
the server responds.

# Parameters

params

: The parameters to send to the client.

  The following fields will be automatically set if left blank.

  - `process_id`: Set to the PID of the current process.

# Returns

InitializeResult

: The result received from the client.

methods.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient.shutdown_session
documentation:
Shutdown the server under test.

Helper method that handles sending `shutdown` and `exit` messages in the
correct order.

:::{note}
This method will not attempt to send these messages if a fatal error has
occurred.
:::

methods.name: pytest_lsp.client.LanguageClient.wait_for_notification
documentation:
Block until a notification with the given method is received.

Parameters
----------
method
   The notification method to wait for, e.g. ``textDocument/publishDiagnostics``
Used to drive language servers under test.
